translated: One evening, after they had finished eating together, Jesus said to them, "You must love one another."

ref: Then Jesus said, ‚ÄúHow much bread do you have? Go and see!‚Äù They found out and answered, ‚ÄúWe have five small loaves of bread and two fish.‚Äù
text: Ni di Yesu lat…îk…î ma al…õ, ‚ÄúBit…î…îky…õ bi…õlaanyu bil…õ bloblo akudi as…õ ninte ye?‚Äù Baakple baabat…îk…î nw…î al…õ, ‚ÄúBloblo akudi an…î…î ku k…îkpaku s…õnle k…îny…î ko kule.‚Äù
translated: One of Jesus‚Äô disciples asked him, ‚ÄúHow many times should we forgive someone who wrongs us?‚Äù Jesus replied, ‚ÄúNot just seven times, but seventy times seven.‚Äù

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Yesu lay…î bloblo akudi an…î…î ku k…îkpaku nwu k…îny…î, ni aanyu osi aakus…õkus…õsa nya. Aab…õb…õ…õ bloblo nwu aata basaateketet…õ n…î…î al…õ bas…õ bata batii nwu, ni aab…õb…õ…õ k…îkpaku nwu k…îny…î kaf…î…î aata batii nwu bamuu n…õ.
translated: Jesus gave his life as food and drink for us, so that we may always remember him. We eat his life with the bread that we break, and we drink his blood with the wine that we share together.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Obiala l…õma lale aami…õ.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Nni kamaa, Yesu omu kaf…î…î lasifi kabokotee osi aalaap…õ …îlaa aata Yaa.
translated: That night, Jesus took bread, broke it and gave it to his disciples saying, "This is my body."

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Ni aayie aabuo di oklo nwu kam…õ di anu l…õma, ni di…õ af…õ…õf…î nwu ladi…õ …îf…õ n…õ. Diat…õ diabla ma …îkp…õ osie kanya!
translated: If you want something good to happen to you, you must also do good to others. Don't expect blessings if you do evil!

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.

translated: There is a promise that whoever believes in Jesus will have eternal life.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Kak…î lele di Yesu l…õky…õ, nkpayet≈© woo, sekpoo woo, ee sefensuku, mank…õnk…õ bafi…õt…õ mamat…õ di asaa kayak…î aba aba, mantikiti nw…î lekoto al…õ, ata bafi…õt…õ nwu bakpanko awu n…î…î kotoko. Batii ba niakpanko nw…î ni awu kotoko, sifi…õ lay…î…î ma.
translated: When Jesus was crucified, there was pain, there was suffering, and on the third day, the women went to the tomb to see if Jesus had risen, but they found the stone rolled away, and the tomb was empty. While they were wondering about this, two men in shining clothes stood beside them.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Baafuo otofo ta Farisi batii ku Yuda batii lat…î…îbla bawa l…õma amanle ya baadi…õ baat…õ ma nii. Nioso diediki beefoto nn…õ…õ l…õma f…õ mmle baatuo ma nii, fi…õ manle alesaa.
translated: Some Pharisees and teachers of the law came from Jerusalem to Jesus and asked him, "Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They don't wash their hands before they eat."
